name: Dim 03 — Sample size and statistical power
what_it_solves: Full moat_comparison dimension #3: 'Sample size and statistical power' — Bev2026 approach vs CrisPRO approach with verbatim quote.
how_it_solves: Extracted verbatim from moat_comparison.comparison_dimensions[i]; raw dict carried in attributes.raw.
why_it_exists: Depth pass: previous stream flattened each dimension to a few fields. This entity carries every field the JSON contains for that dimension.

attributes.moat_status: DIFFERENT KIND OF STATISTICAL POWER. Bev2026 has high biological replicate quality (n=6 per condition, paired isogenic) with low N. CrisPRO has high N (1000+ cell lines) with single-readout pharmacology. Best practice combines both.
attributes.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF figure legends. CrisPRO from canonical receipts.

raw.Bev2026_n: 2 isogenic clone pairs (HT1197 + T24) + 2 PDOs (TN-001 + BlCa197) + 2 cross-tumor models (SW1990 + SPC111). Total isogenic readout systems: 4 cell line pairs + 2 PDO pairs = 6 paired systems.
raw.CrisPRO_n_for_MBD4_ATR_axis: n_LOF=14, n_WT=914 (Methods rule). Plus DepMap CRISPR n=1186 lines for gene-effect dependency.
raw.CrisPRO_comparator_pool_for_PARP1_expres: n=19/1498 (broader because expression data covers more lines than drug-screen data)
raw.moat_status: DIFFERENT KIND OF STATISTICAL POWER. Bev2026 has high biological replicate quality (n=6 per condition, paired isogenic) with low N. CrisPRO has high N (1000+ cell lines) with single-readout pharmacology. Best practice combines both.
raw.verification_status: Bev2026 from PDF figure legends. CrisPRO from canonical receipts.

verbatim_evidence.note: verbatim quote from Bev2026 PDF as recorded at file creation time
